summary: This note is for new readers who want a clear first mental model of the simulator.
Explain what this simulator is trying to do.

section: What is a simulator trying to do
summary: At a high level, this simulator predicts lap behavior from a set of assumptions and parameters.
Quasi static means we model each local point as if it is close to equilibrium.
Steady state means force and moment balances are solved for a point as if the state is not rapidly changing during that solve.

section: Two key inputs you will see everywhere

section: Why the black line can look lower than every pass

section: What happens near the start and end points
summary: Forward and backward passes start from different ends of the point list.
Introduces pass-by-pass mechanics for cornering, acceleration, and braking limits.
Pass 1 solves each point for the highest feasible corner speed.
Pass 2 walks from start to end.
Pass 3 walks from end to start.
The final speed profile takes the minimum of forward and backward constrained profiles.
